reasoning: Joe Biden had a massively high search volume in 2024 (due to the US election and his withdrawal), meaning his baseline for the 'trending' metric—which measures the spike in traffic in 2025 compared to 2024—is extremely high. Now out of office, it is highly unlikely his 2025 searches will spike above 2024 levels, let alone enough to make him one of the top trending figures globally. In addition, the market requires him to land exactly at the #2 spot, which further decreases the probability against a field of strong contenders with massive 2025 spikes (like Pope Leo XIV). The probability is set to the lowest allowed limit taking these compounding factors into account.
